name: Horace Silver
abstract: Horace Ward Martin Tavares Silver (September 2, 1928 – June 18, 2014) was an American jazz pianist, composer, and arranger, particularly in the hard bop style that he helped pioneer in the 1950s. After playing tenor saxophone and piano at school in Connecticut, Silver got his break on piano when his trio was recruited by Stan Getz in 195…

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Charlie Parker Memorial Concert
Newport Jazz Festival: Art Blakey and The Jazz Messengers/ Horace Silver Quintet/ Freddie Hubbard

about_quotes.speaker: Sam Jones
about_quotes.evidence: The only mention of Horace Silver is by the [INTERVIEWER] stating 'Horace's band was a good band.' The interviewee, Sam Jones, does not respond to this specific mention with any personal anecdote, confirmation of working together, or statement of acquaintance.

about_quotes.speaker: Art Farmer
about_quotes.evidence: Horace got his own group and had me in the group. Me and Hank Mobley was in the group... I had recorded with Horace. I had used him on some of my records
about_quotes.doc_title: Art Farmer — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Randy Brecker
about_quotes.evidence: I announced that I was going to join Horace Silver... I’m making two fifty a week with Horace... Horace stopped the take and made us start over.
about_quotes.doc_title: Randy Brecker interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 12, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Louis Hayes
about_quotes.evidence: I did five with him before I left in ’59. And I could really see my growth and listen and hear my growth when I was appearing with Horace.
about_quotes.doc_title: Louis Hayes interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 29, 2000

about_quotes.speaker: Reggie Watkins
about_quotes.evidence: Roger Humphries, who is the great — he’s like the Godfather of jazz in Pittsburgh. He played with Horace Silver.
about_quotes.doc_title: Reggie Watkins interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, May 22, 2018

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Cranshaw
about_quotes.evidence: Bob has performed with Lee Morgan, Wes Montgomery, Horace Silver, Ella Fitzgerald and Joe Williams
about_quotes.doc_title: Bob Cranshaw interviewed by Michael Woods, New York City, New York, October 18, 1995
